attributes.name: motivated
attributes.rationale: Ready to act now. Prioritize with specific, low-friction next steps.

attributes.name: self_involved
attributes.rationale: Status-centered framing. Keep engagement brief unless stronger intent appears.

attributes.name: negative_outlook
attributes.rationale: Pessimistic tone. Acknowledge first, then gently reframe toward agency.

attributes.name: fan
attributes.rationale: High affinity and goodwill. Useful for rapport and lightweight engagement.

attributes.name: introvert
attributes.rationale: Reflective communication style. Prefer thoughtful prompts over hard push.

approaches.name: ignore
approaches.rationale: Skip low-signal/noise comments to conserve effort for actionable conversations.

approaches.name: encourage
approaches.rationale: Affirm momentum and reinforce identity shift. Best for growth-oriented or positive comments.

approaches.name: intrigue
approaches.rationale: Use curiosity/open loops to continue conversation when intent is emerging but not explicit.

approaches.name: inquire
approaches.rationale: Lead with a clarifying question to diagnose context before advising. Best for pain/question comments.

approaches.name: direct_cta
approaches.rationale: Offer one concrete next step when intent is explicit and friction is low.

rulesGuides.text: Never reuse the exact same reply text across comments
Usually anchor response to the specific comment text
Don't ask pointless follow up questions as if we are working on a project together
Avoid generic coaching templates
Tie the comment into the reference video if it works
Don't shoehorn in keywords - every statement should be coherent
Refer it to ISITAS, the ISIT Construct, OR the ISIT Game, as appropriate
Capitalize sentences. (except for every now and then to simulate human error)

categories.name: socioeconomic
categories.rationale: Describes struggle, friction, or need for help.

categories.name: metacrisis
categories.rationale: Shows openness to change or leveling up.

categories.name: ai alignment
categories.rationale: Direct question or asks for direction.

categories.name: crypto
categories.rationale: ISITAS will be blockchain based with our own native crypto token economy. Indeed, ISITAS has the potential to save the crypto space by giving it a worthy purpose and a platform on which to build some actual value on which a crypto project could be based.

categories.name: post-labor economics
categories.rationale: How are humans going to earn a living when AI and robotics can surpass our performance?

categories.name: game theory
categories.rationale: The ISIT Game is a collaborative, infinite game designed to harness the power of swarm intelligence to solve the greatest challenges facing humanity including AI Alignment, post-labor economics, wealth inequality, war and violence, climate change, and more. Game theory is one of the most powerful forces in the universe, and this project has tapped into it directly.

categories.name: channeling
categories.rationale: Channeling spiritual messages about personal or societal events.

categories.name: spirituality
categories.rationale: Expressing belief in spirituality as the ultimate solution to problems.

categories.name: general
categories.rationale: Low-actionability baseline. Deprioritize unless paired with strong attributes.

categories.name: current events
categories.rationale: Just commenting on/complaining about current events with no solution-orientation.

categories.name: Universal Basic Income (UBI)
categories.rationale: Interested in equity, sustainability, basic human needs, and related issues.

categories.name: politics
categories.rationale: Overtly discussing politics, taking sides, and advocating for political solutions. We generally want to avoid overtly political discussion at this point.

attributes.name: motivated
attributes.rationale: Ready to act now. Prioritize with specific, low-friction next steps.

attributes.name: self-involved
attributes.rationale: Focuses on self-image/status over substance. Telling us about all their plans to do something important one day, but meanwhile they have many obstacles in their way which they are happy to share about with random strangers in Youtube comments.

attributes.name: pessimistic
attributes.rationale: Language indicates defeatism/cynicism. Sees doom and gloom ahead.

attributes.name: fan
attributes.rationale: High affinity and goodwill. Useful for rapport and lightweight engagement.

attributes.name: introvert
attributes.rationale: Reflective communication style. Prefer thoughtful prompts over hard push.

attributes.name: Goal-oriented
attributes.rationale: We are currently seeking people who are serious about actually doing something to solve the problems of the world. It seems like the majority just want to talk about the issues endlessly, but the people who are actually offering solutions are few and far between. We're looking for signals from people who are willing to take action if they only know how.

attributes.name: attention seeker
attributes.rationale: They are just showing off how smart or creative they are, trying to impress people.

attributes.name: chosen one
attributes.rationale: Compelling sense of purpose. Feelings of being special. Gifted. Crystal children. Light workers.

attributes.name: suspicious
attributes.rationale: Expressing distrust of authorities or others in general. Tendency toward conspiracy theories.

attributes.name: shallow
attributes.rationale: Repeating surface level commentary just for the sake of participating and going along with the crowd.

attributes.name: solution-oriented
attributes.rationale: Interested in getting to actual solutions, even if they don't know what they are yet.

attributes.name: optimistic
attributes.rationale: Sees a brighter future ahead. People are basically good. We are capable of solving our problems.

attributes.name: engaged
attributes.rationale: Makes comments about how they have actually done things or are doing things in the real world to proactively solve problems, not just talking about it.

attributes.name: intelligent
attributes.rationale: Offers original, well-thought out and articulated thoughts that add new information or clarify confusion.

attributes.name: analytical
attributes.rationale: Focusing on the details. Looking at the mechanics of the situation.

attributes.name: npc
attributes.rationale: Non-performing character. Not saying anything of significance.

attributes.name: bitter
attributes.rationale: Negative overall attitude

attributes.name: clever
attributes.rationale: Pithy quips. Getting straight to the key point. Noticing a different angle.

approaches.name: ignore
approaches.rationale: Skip low-signal/noise comments to conserve effort for actionable conversations.

approaches.name: encourage
approaches.rationale: Affirm momentum and reinforce identity shift. Best for growth-oriented or positive comments.

approaches.name: intrigue
approaches.rationale: Use curiosity/open loops to continue conversation when intent is emerging but not explicit.

approaches.name: inquire
approaches.rationale: Lead with a clarifying question to diagnose context before advising. Best for pain/question comments.

approaches.name: respond
approaches.rationale: React to some specific point they made or question they asked.

approaches.name: quip
approaches.rationale: Say something short and clever that ties what they said in with the video

approaches.name: agree
approaches.rationale: Validate their position or statements with solid reasoning, and throw in a compliment

approaches.name: relate
approaches.rationale: Give an example that makes their point, share a similar experience, empathize.

approaches.name: invitation
approaches.rationale: Tell them they are the kind of people we are looking for and invite them to discover ISITAS.

rulesGuides.text: Never reuse the exact same reply text across comments
Usually anchor response to the specific comment text
Don't ask pointless follow up questions as if we are working on a project together
Avoid generic coaching templates
Tie the comment into the reference video if it works
Don't shoehorn in keywords - every statement should be coherent
Refer it to ISITAS, the ISIT Construct, OR the ISIT Game, as appropriate
Capitalize sentences. (except for every now and then to simulate human error)
